channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
item.description: 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.
item.content:encoded:
15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U.S. to the DRC are now living in uncertainty in a country an with ongoing armed conflict, where they have no ties.

(Image credit: Schalk Van Zuydam)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: Lawsuits accuse State Farm of secretly working to cut insurance payouts
item.description: Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.
item.content:encoded:
Lawsuits allege that State Farm tries to avoid paying what it owes for hail damage. The litigation is happening as homeowners face soaring insurance costs, partly due to threats from climate change.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: King Charles argues for stronger cooperation in speech to a joint meeting of Congress
item.description: "The challenges we face are too great for any one nation to bear alone," King Charles told lawmakers Tuesday as part of a visit to mark the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence from Great Britain.
item.content:encoded:
"The challenges we face are too great for any one nation to bear alone," King Charles told lawmakers Tuesday as part of a visit to mark the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence from Great Britain.

(Image credit: Pool)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Suspect charged with trying to assassinate President Trump, Trump hosts King Charles at critical point in U.S.-Britain relations, ceasefire in south Lebanon fraying.
item.content:encoded: Suspect charged with trying to assassinate President Trump, Trump hosts King Charles at critical point in U.S.-Britain relations, ceasefire in south Lebanon fraying.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Correspondents' dinner suspect charged with trying to assassinate President Trump
item.description: The Justice Department announced the first formal charges against the suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department announced the first formal charges against the suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Former Homeland Security official on how to protect the president at events
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks former Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about security needs and constraints when protecting President Trump during an event like Saturday's dinner.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks former Homeland Security official Juliette Kayyem about security needs and constraints when protecting President Trump during an event like Saturday's dinner.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: SCOTUS justices appear divided on Monsanto weedkiller case
item.description: Supreme Court justices heard arguments about liability for the maker of the popular weed killer Roundup and whether federal law should preempt state lawsuits over health troubles.
item.content:encoded: Supreme Court justices heard arguments about liability for the maker of the popular weed killer Roundup and whether federal law should preempt state lawsuits over health troubles.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: George Washington's lost pistol is now at the Smithsonian
item.description: NPR's America in Pursuit series examines a presidential treasure, a pistol that was lost by George Washington and now can be found at the Smithsonian.
item.content:encoded: NPR's America in Pursuit series examines a presidential treasure, a pistol that was lost by George Washington and now can be found at the Smithsonian.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong

item.title: Some lawmakers say correspondents' dinner attack raises questions over Secret Service
item.description: Lawmakers say the thwarted attack on the White House Correspondents' Association dinner raises questions about Secret Service protection. Some say it highlights the need for a White House ballroom.
item.content:encoded: Lawmakers say the thwarted attack on the White House Correspondents' Association dinner raises questions about Secret Service protection. Some say it highlights the need for a White House ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: College classmate speaks about alleged correspondents' dinner attacker
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Eliza Terlinden, who was in the same Christian fellowship group in college as the suspected attacker at the White House Correspondents dinner.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to Eliza Terlinden, who was in the same Christian fellowship group in college as the suspected attacker at the White House Correspondents dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
